varieties.name: Anna
varieties.traits: Hybrid - cross developed specifically for low-chill climates; Medium to large fruit, greenish-yellow background with red blush (50% coverage), crisp white flesh
varieties.flesh_fruit: Creamy-white flesh, sweet and slightly tart, similar to Red Delicious when ripe. Flavor improves when allowed to ripen on tree
varieties.yield_notes: Very productive, often fruits in 1st-2nd year. Upright to semi-spreading tree, medium height
varieties.availability: Daleys Fruit Tree Nursery, Ladybird Nursery humid subtropical climates, Fruit Tree Cottage, Woodbridge Fruit Trees, PlantNet Australia

varieties.name: Dorsett Golden
varieties.origin: Bahamas, early 1950s (discovered by Mrs. Dorsett as a chance seedling)
varieties.traits: Hybrid - believed to have Golden Delicious in parentage; Medium to large fruit, yellow-green skin with faint red-orange blush, firm white flesh
varieties.flesh_fruit: Sweet, firm, crisp white flesh similar to Golden Delicious but sweeter and more flavorful. Low acidity
varieties.yield_notes: High yielding, very precocious—fruits in 1st-2nd year. Semi-spreading tree
varieties.availability: Daleys Fruit Tree Nursery, Ladybird Nursery humid subtropical climates, Queensland nurseries

varieties.name: Tropic Sweet
varieties.traits: Hybrid - specifically developed for tropical and subtropical climates; Medium-sized fruit, light green with 30% red blush, firm texture. Some skin russeting may occur
varieties.flesh_fruit: Very sweet (14-15° Brix), firm, crisp, low acid. Similar to Royal Gala in flavor. White flesh

varieties.name: Granny Smith 🏆
varieties.origin: Australia, Eastwood NSW, 1868 (discovered by Maria Ann Smith)
varieties.traits: Heirloom - chance seedling, thought to be hybrid of European wild apple and French Crab apple; Large, bright green fruit with prominent white lenticels, hard and firm
varieties.flesh_fruit: Greenish-white flesh, very crisp, juicy, tart and acidic. Classic cooking apple flavor
varieties.yield_notes: Heavy bearer, vigorous grower, long-lived tree
varieties.availability: Woodbridge Fruit Trees, Heritage Fruit Trees, Daleys, most Australian nurseries

varieties.name: Pink Lady (Cripps Pink) 🧬
varieties.origin: Western Australia, 1973 (bred by John Cripps), released 1984
varieties.traits: Hybrid - cross between Golden Delicious and Lady Williams; Medium to large, ellipsoid shape, distinctive pink blush over green/yellow background
varieties.flesh_fruit: Pale yellow, firm, crisp flesh with balanced sweet-tart flavor, aromatic
varieties.availability: PlantNet Australia, Woodbridge Fruit Trees, Daleys (as Pinkabelle dwarf)

varieties.name: Lady Williams 🏆
varieties.origin: Western Australia, 1930s (discovered by Maud Williams), released commercially 1968
varieties.traits: Heirloom - chance seedling, likely from Granny Smith x Rokewood or Jonathan; Medium to large, pink-red coloring, elongated conical shape
varieties.flesh_fruit: Creamy white, firm, crisp flesh with sweet-tart balance
varieties.yield_notes: Good productivity, can be biennial. Parent of Pink Lady and Sundowner
varieties.availability: Woodbridge Fruit Trees, Heritage Fruit Trees Australia, Daleys

varieties.name: Sundowner (Cripps Red) 🧬
varieties.origin: Western Australia, 1970s (bred by John Cripps), also marketed as Joya
varieties.traits: Hybrid - cross between Golden Delicious and Lady Williams (sibling to Pink Lady); Small to medium, bi-colored red over yellow-green base, round to oblong
varieties.flesh_fruit: Pale yellow to ivory, dense, firm, crisp with sweet-tart balance. More pronounced flavor than Pink Lady
varieties.availability: Daleys Fruit Tree Nursery, Woodbridge Fruit Trees, humid subtropical climates specialist nurseries

varieties.name: Fuji
varieties.origin: Japan, 1960s (cross between Red Delicious and Ralls Janet)
varieties.traits: Hybrid; Large, round, yellow-green background with red stripes and blushing
varieties.flesh_fruit: Dense, very sweet (high sugar content 15-18%), crisp, juicy. Excellent fresh eating
varieties.availability: humid subtropical climates Plant Nursery, Daleys, Woodbridge Fruit Trees, Queensland nurseries

varieties.name: Gala
varieties.origin: New Zealand, 1930s (cross between Kidd's Orange Red and Golden Delicious)
varieties.traits: Hybrid; Medium, yellow-orange skin with red stripes and blush
varieties.flesh_fruit: Crisp, aromatic, sweet with hints of vanilla and citrus, low acidity
varieties.availability: Daleys, Woodbridge Fruit Trees, Queensland nurseries

varieties.name: Royal Gala
varieties.origin: New Zealand, sport of Gala, named by Queen Elizabeth II
varieties.traits: Hybrid - mutation of Gala; Similar to Gala, more intense red coloring
varieties.flesh_fruit: Sweet, crisp, aromatic like Gala

varieties.name: Golden Delicious
varieties.traits: Heirloom - chance seedling; Medium to large, greenish-gold to yellow skin
varieties.flesh_fruit: Creamy yellow, very juicy, sweet, mellow flavor. Excellent all-purpose
varieties.yield_notes: Productive, good pollinator for other varieties

varieties.name: Jonathan
varieties.traits: Heirloom; Medium, bright red skin
varieties.flesh_fruit: White to cream, crisp, juicy, sweet-tart balance, aromatic

varieties.name: Red Delicious
varieties.traits: Heirloom; Large, distinctive elongated heart shape, deep red color
varieties.flesh_fruit: White, crisp when fresh, mildly sweet

varieties.name: Jonagold
varieties.origin: New York State, USA, 1968 (cross between Jonathan and Golden Delicious)
varieties.traits: Hybrid; Large, yellow-green background with red blush
varieties.flesh_fruit: Yellow, firm, crisp, juicy, sweet-tart balance

varieties.name: Pinkabelle 🧬
varieties.origin: Western Australia (dwarf sport of Cripps Pink)
varieties.traits: Hybrid - compact dwarf form of Pink Lady; Compact, spur-bearing dwarf, produces full-sized Pink Lady fruit
varieties.flesh_fruit: Same as Pink Lady - firm, sweet-tart, aromatic
varieties.yield_notes: Good for compact size, early bearing
varieties.availability: PlantNet Australia, Daleys, Woodbridge Fruit Trees

varieties.name: Gravenstein
varieties.traits: Heirloom; Large, light green with red stripes
varieties.flesh_fruit: Crisp, juicy, aromatic, balanced sweet-tart with honey notes

varieties.name: Anna
varieties.traits: Hybrid; Medium to large, greenish-yellow with red blush
varieties.flesh_fruit: Sweet, crisp, white flesh

varieties.name: Dorsett Golden
varieties.traits: Hybrid; Yellow-green with red-orange blush
varieties.flesh_fruit: Sweet, firm, crisp
varieties.availability: Miracle Farm Kattappana, tropical monsoon climates agricultural research stations, tropical fruit nurseries

varieties.name: HRMN-99 (Hariman Selection)
varieties.origin: Himachal Pradesh, India, discovered c.2010 by Hariman Sharma
varieties.traits: Hybrid - specially selected for tropical/subtropical cultivation; Green to greenish-yellow fruit, medium size
varieties.flesh_fruit: Sweet, juicy, good flavor
varieties.yield_notes: High productivity, can fruit in 1st year

varieties.name: Granny Smith
varieties.traits: Heirloom; Large, bright green
varieties.flesh_fruit: Tart, crisp, juicy
varieties.availability: Miracle Farm Kattappana, specialized agricultural programs in tropical monsoon climates

varieties.name: Red Delicious
varieties.traits: Heirloom; Large, deep red, heart-shaped
varieties.flesh_fruit: Mildly sweet, crisp when fresh

varieties.name: Ein Shemer
varieties.origin: Israel (Ein Shemer kibbutz), bred for hot arid climates
varieties.traits: Hybrid; Small to medium, green to golden-yellow
varieties.flesh_fruit: Sweet, tender, aromatic

varieties.name: Tropical Beauty
varieties.origin: South Africa, c.1930, introduced to Australia 1958
varieties.traits: Open-pollinated selection for tropical conditions; Medium to large, round, dark red over green-yellow base
varieties.flesh_fruit: Crisp, sweet, dessert quality
varieties.availability: Miracle Farm Kattappana, Kanthallur regional nurseries, tropical monsoon climates agricultural stations

varieties.name: Shell of Alabama
varieties.traits: Open-pollinated variety; Medium, good appearance
varieties.flesh_fruit: Balanced flavor, good for fresh eating and cooking
varieties.availability: Specialist nurseries, may require special order from international sources

varieties.name: Akane
varieties.origin: Japan (cross involving Worcester Pearmain and Jonathan)
varieties.traits: Hybrid; Medium, red coloring, resembles Worcester Pearmain
varieties.flesh_fruit: Balanced flavor influenced by Jonathan parent
varieties.yield_notes: Good, apples stay on tree longer allowing extended harvest

varieties.name: Israeli Varieties Group: Elah, Maayan, Michal, Shlomit
varieties.traits: Hybrid varieties from Israeli breeding programs; Various sizes and colors, all heat-adapted
varieties.flesh_fruit: Good eating quality, balanced flavors
varieties.availability: Limited in India, may require special import arrangements, contact agricultural research stations

varieties.name: Beverly Hills
varieties.traits: Open-pollinated low-chill variety; Green fruit, medium size
varieties.flesh_fruit: Good eating quality

varieties.name: Gordon
varieties.traits: Open-pollinated variety; Medium, adapted to warm climates
varieties.flesh_fruit: Good quality

varieties.name: Winter Banana
varieties.traits: Heirloom variety; Large, pale yellow with pink blush, distinctive appearance
varieties.flesh_fruit: Mild flavor with banana-like aroma, tender

varieties.name: Pettingill
varieties.traits: Heirloom - warm climate selection; Medium, adapted specifically for mild winter climates
varieties.flesh_fruit: Good eating quality
